error_signature: Bad Request: bad webhook: Failed to resolve host
title: Telegram Trigger webhook resolution failure
symptom: When testing the Telegram Trigger, n8n throws a 'Bad Request: bad webhook: Failed to resolve host' error.
applies_when: Hosted on a VPS with a custom domain
not_this_if: Using a public domain without DNS issues
Error occurs in a different node
root_cause: The error occurs due to DNS resolution issues where the server cannot resolve the hostname of the webhook URL. This can happen if the DNS settings are misconfigured or if the DNS server being used is unreliable.
fix_steps: Check the DNS setup on your VPS by running 'cat /etc/resolv.conf'.
Update the DNS settings to use reliable DNS servers like Cloudflare (1.1.1.1) and Google (8.8.8.8).
Save the changes and test the DNS resolution with 'ping my-subdomain.duckdns.org' and 'dig my-subdomain.duckdns.org'.
Re-register the Telegram webhook to ensure it points to the correct DuckDNS domain.
agent_instruction: Update the DNS settings on your VPS and re-register the Telegram webhook.
